name: Irish Soda Bread (Abm)
recipeIngredient: 2/3 cup Raisins (add a few more as desired)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the bread flour, sugar, salt, butter (or oil), raisins, caraway seeds, baking soda, buttermilk, and dry yeast.
2. Stir until well combined and form into a dough.
3. Place the dough on a floured surface and knead briefly to bring it together.
4. Shape the dough into a round loaf and place in a greased 9x5 inch loaf pan.
5. Allow the dough to rise until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
6. Punch down the dough and shape it into a loaf.
7. Place the loaf back in the pan and let rise for an additional 30 minutes.
8. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
9. Bake the bread for 25-30 minutes or until golden brown on top.
10. Remove from the oven and allow to cool before slicing and serving.
